questions.text: Which of the following is a vascular cryptogam?
questions.explanation: Pteridophytes are vascular cryptogams (have vascular tissue but no seeds). Bryophytes are non-vascular.

questions.text: The plant body in mosses is:
questions.explanation: In bryophytes (mosses), the dominant plant body is the gametophyte (haploid), unlike vascular plants where sporophyte is dominant.

questions.text: Gymnosperms are plants with naked seeds.
questions.explanation: Gymnosperms have naked seeds not enclosed in fruit. The term 'gymnosperm' means 'naked seed'.

questions.text: The male sex organ in bryophytes is called ________.
questions.explanation: Antheridium is the male sex organ in bryophytes and pteridophytes. It produces flagellated antherozoids (male gametes).
